<function=python>
<parameter=code>

        break

        print(f"Path (id=6): color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary'][:4]}...")
        break
</parameter>

The player moved from cols 14-18 to cols 20-24 (6 columns right). The path (node 6) is still visible as a black object. The path boundary starts at [[7, 13], [7, 43], [13, 43], [13, 42]]... which traces the full maze route.
Now I need to understand the maze connectivity. The player is at rows 8-12, cols 20-24. The path goes right to col 43, then down.
Let me continue RIGHT to reach the right side, then figure out how to go DOWN.

Let me think about the path:
- Start: top-left (rows 8-12, cols 14-18)
- Go RIGHT to reach the right side (cols 38-42)
- Then go DOWN along the right side
- Then go LEFT along the bottom
- Then go UP to reach the target
But earlier, DOWN was blocked from the top corridor. Maybe I need to reach a specific position where DOWN is possible.
Let me continue RIGHT and try DOWN at each position to find where the passage opens up.
Actually, let me reconsider. The path boundary shows:
[7, 13] -> [7, 43]: top horizontal
[7, 43] -> [13, 43]: right vertical (short)
[13, 43] -> [13, 42]: slight left
[13, 42] -> [14, 41]: diagonal
[14, 41] -> [41, 41]: long vertical down
[41, 41] -> [41, 20]: bottom horizontal left
[41, 20] -> [42, 19]: slight down-left
[42, 19] -> [48, 19]: vertical down
[48, 19] -> [49, 20]: slight down-right
[49, 20] -> [49, 41]: bottom horizontal right
[49, 41] -> [48, 42]: slight up-right
[48, 42] -> [48, 50]: horizontal right
[48, 50] -> [56, 50]: vertical down
[56, 50] -> [56, 42]: horizontal left
[56, 42] -> [55, 41]: slight up-left
[55, 41] -> [55, 13]: horizontal left back to start
Wait, this path seems to trace the OUTER boundary of the maze structure, not an inner path. The maze is like a frame or border, and the path goes around it.
Actually, I think the path IS the walkable corridor. The player moves along this black corridor. The white areas are "holes" or "rooms" inside the maze structure.
So the player needs to:
1. Go RIGHT along the top corridor to reach the right side
2. Go DOWN along the right corridor
3. Go LEFT along the bottom corridor
4. Go UP along the left corridor (or reach the target)
But the target is at bottom-right, not top-left. So maybe the path is:
1. Go RIGHT to the right side
2. Go DOWN to the bottom
3. The target is at the bottom-right area
Let me continue RIGHT and then try DOWN.
